root_text.sn56.37:1.1: “Sūriyassa, bhikkhave, udayato etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—aruṇuggaṁ.
root_text.sn56.37:1.2: Evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno catunnaṁ ariyasaccānaṁ yathābhūtaṁ abhisamayāya etaṁ pubbaṅgamaṁ etaṁ pubbanimittaṁ, yadidaṁ—sammādiṭṭhi.
root_text.sn56.37:1.4: ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānissati …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yathābhūtaṁ pajānissati.
root_text.sn56.37:2.1: Tasmātiha, bhikkhave, ‘idaṁ dukkhan’ti yogo karaṇīyo …pe… ‘ayaṁ dukkhanirodhagāminī paṭipadā’ti yogo karaṇīyo”ti.

translation_text.sn56.37:1.1: “Mendicants, the dawn is the forerunner and precursor of the sunrise.
translation_text.sn56.37:1.2: In the same way, right view is the forerunner and precursor of truly comprehending the four noble truths.
translation_text.sn56.37:1.3: A mendicant with right view can expect to truly understand:
translation_text.sn56.37:1.4: ‘This is suffering’ … ‘This is the origin of suffering’ … ‘This is the cessation of suffering’ … ‘This is the practice that leads to the cessation of suffering’.
translation_text.sn56.37:2.1: That’s why you should practice meditation …”
